location.description: Where the error occurred, e.g. 'body.items[3].tags' or 'path.thing-id'

$schema.description: A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.
examples: Property foo is required but is missing.

errors.description: Optional list of individual error details

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type. This value should not change between occurrences of the error.

type.description: A URI reference to human-readable documentation for the error.

events.description: Event types to deliver. Empty = all events.

headers.description: Custom request headers. Values may use {{variable}} substitution.

payloadTemplate.description: Body template with {{variable}} substitution. Empty = default JSON envelope.

secret.description: HMAC-SHA256 signing key. Leave empty on update to keep the current secret.

$schema.description: A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.

drumCount.description: Number of drums on the winch

labelAuto.description: Whether the label is auto-generated from position

station.description: Which deck the winch is on

worstStatus.description: Worst condition among the lines on this winch

apiKey.description: API key issued per user. Send as `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. `X-API-Key: <key>` and `?api_key=<key>` are also accepted.

info.description:
Track mooring lines across their life: where each line sits on deck, its
identity and certification, side-tracking and turning, inspections, condition rollups, and
document/photo evidence.

**Deployment scope.** The same API runs in two scopes, set by configuration, not by the client:
- *onboard* — one vessel; the deployment ignores any other vessel id you send and serves its own.
- *shore* — the whole fleet; vessel id selects which vessel.

**Authentication.** Every endpoint except `/health` requires an API key. Send it as
`Authorization: Bearer <key>` (preferred), `X-API-Key: <key>`, or `?api_key=<key>`
(handy for file downloads). Keys are issued per user.

**Condition.** Line/winch/storage condition is one of `Good`, `Monitor`, or `Action`.

**Integrating an inspection provider.**
1. Push assessments with `POST /inspections/ingest` — look the line up by `serialNumber`,
   and pass an `externalId` so retries are idempotent.
2. Comment on an existing inspection with `POST /inspections/{id}/feedback`.
3. Subscribe to changes with webhooks (`/webhooks`); deliveries are HMAC-SHA256 signed.

**Errors.** Failures return a JSON problem document (`status`, `title`, `detail`, and a
field-level `errors` array on validation problems).
info.title: Mooring Line Management API

post.description: The inbound integration endpoint for external inspection providers. Resolves the line by `serialNumber`, records the assessment, and updates the line's current condition. Idempotent by `externalId`: replaying the same key returns the stored inspection with `created:false`. Returns `{ inspection, created }`. Fires `inspection.logged` only on first insert.
post.summary: Ingest an inspection from a third-party system

get.description: Returns inspections across a vessel (newest first), each enriched with the line's name and serial. On a shore deployment, omit `vesselId` for a fleet-wide log.

parameters.description: Vessel to scope to; ignored onboard (always the configured vessel)
parameters.name: vesselId

schema.description: Vessel to scope to; ignored onboard (always the configured vessel)

get.summary: Chronological inspection logbook for a vessel

get.description: Returns every feedback entry attached to an inspection, oldest first. An unknown inspection id yields an empty list.

post.description: Records a follow-up assessment or acknowledgement on an existing inspection — the channel a third-party reviewer uses after consuming an `inspection.logged` event. Idempotent by `externalId` (replaying the same key returns the stored feedback with `created:false`). Feedback is an annotation: it does not change the line's condition. Fires the `inspection.feedback` webhook event.

get.description: Functional line categories (e.g. mooring tail, main line, lashing).
Returns a line's complete record: identity, certification, specs (inherited from its product), location, side-tracking ages, and condition.

post.summary: Upload a certificate, manual or guide for a line

post.description: Registers a sub-line (such as a tail or lashing) under a parent line, sharing its vessel. Same body as register-line.
post.summary: Add a component (tail/lashing) to a line

get.description: Returns a line's inspections, most recent first.

post.description: Records a condition assessment against a line and updates the line's current condition. Use this for crew/manual entries; third-party systems should use `POST /inspections/ingest`. Fires the `inspection.logged` webhook event.
post.summary: Log a manual inspection for a line

post.description: Relocates a line. Provide exactly one of `toDrumId` or `toStorageId`; sending both or neither is rejected. Moving onto an occupied drum is a conflict. Fires `line.moved`.
post.summary: Move a line to a drum or to storage

get.summary: List condition photos for a line

post.summary: Upload a condition photo for a line
Turn a line to its other side

get.description: Catalogue products (a maker + line type with specs). Lines are registered from these.
Returns one catalogue product with its full specs.
Returns a downloadable report of every top-level line's latest condition. `format=csv` (default) or `format=pdf`. For programmatic reads prefer the JSON list endpoints.

parameters.description: Vessel to scope to; ignored onboard
parameters.name: vesselId

schema.description: Vessel to scope to; ignored onboard

get.summary: Download a condition report (CSV or PDF)
List a user's API keys (metadata only)

post.summary: Issue an API key (plaintext returned once)

get.description: Returns vessels visible to this deployment. Onboard, that is the single configured vessel; shore returns the fleet.

post.description: Registers a vessel (shore use). Onboard deployments serve a single pre-provisioned vessel.

get.description: Returns one vessel's identity. Onboard, any id resolves to the configured vessel.

Returns the vessel's deck plan: winches with their drums and storage areas, each carrying the worst-case condition of the lines it holds. Winch and storage positions are normalized (x,y in 0..1) against a 1000x600 deck. Pass `?thumbnails=true` to also receive, per winch, a base64 SVG `thumbnail` of that winch highlighted on the deck.

parameters.description: When true, each winch carries a base64 SVG data-URI thumbnail of itself highlighted on the deck
parameters.name: thumbnails

schema.description: When true, each winch carries a base64 SVG data-URI thumbnail of itself highlighted on the deck

get.summary: Get deck layout (winches, drums, storage with worst-case status)

put.description: Replaces the vessel's winches and storage areas in one call (the staged-edit save from the deck editor). Positions are normalized (x,y in 0..1). Fires `layout.updated`.

get.description: Returns the vessel's top-level mooring lines as compact rows, with `items` and a `total` count for paging. Combine filters freely; `q` matches name/serial/tag.

parameters.description: Filter to one line type (see GET /line-types)
parameters.name: lineTypeId

schema.description: Filter to one line type (see GET /line-types)

parameters.description: installed = on a drum; spare = not currently rigged
parameters.name: placement

schema.description: installed = on a drum; spare = not currently rigged

parameters.description: Free-text search over name, serial, and tag
parameters.name: q

schema.description: Free-text search over name, serial, and tag

post.description: Creates a mooring line from a catalogue product. `serialNumber` must be unique on the vessel. Use `lifecycleStatus=ordered` to pre-register a line before it arrives. Fires `line.registered`.

get.summary: Vessel dashboard overview (KPIs, condition, attention, trend)

get.description: Returns every event a subscription can deliver, with the `{{variables}}` available to its header and payload templates. Deliveries are POSTed as a JSON envelope (`event`, `eventId`, `vesselId`, `aggregate`, `aggregateId`, `createdAt`, `data`) and signed with `X-Signature-256: sha256=<hmac>` when a secret is set.

post.description: Subscribes a URL to events. Leave `events` empty to receive all. Set a `secret` to get HMAC-SHA256 signatures. Onboard, the subscription is scoped to the configured vessel.
Delivers a synthetic `webhook.test` event to the subscription's URL so you can verify reachability, headers, and signature handling before relying on live events.
post.summary: Send a test delivery to a webhook
